title: heliohebe raoulii
intro: Heliohebe raoulii, also known as Raoulicopperflower, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand, belonging to the family Plantaginaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Heliohebe raoulii include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote healing, and protect against infections. Herbal preparations of Heliohebe raoulii can be found in the form of tinctures, infusions, and creams, which are used topically to harness its medicinal properties.
